title: Traits 4
description: Functions can return types that implement a trait using `impl Trait` syntax in the return position.
instructions: Fix the return type of the function to use `impl Trait` syntax.
hints: Use `impl Trait` as the return type to return any type implementing that trait.
The syntax is: fn func() -> impl TraitName { ... }
explanation: The `impl Trait` syntax in return position lets you specify that a function returns some type that implements a trait without naming the concrete type. The caller can only use methods defined by the trait. However, the function must return exactly one concrete type - you can't conditionally return different types with `impl Trait`.
